sample.name: CGELBank 50-sentence interannotator-agreement sample
sample.sampling_frame: Fifty new sentences sampled from the English Web Treebank for the interannotator study reported in Reynolds, Arora, and Schneider 2023.
sample.source: English Web Treebank sentences as used in the CGELBank IAA study
exclusions: This validation record summarizes the published CGELBank IAA study; it does not rerun the scorer.
The record validates trained-human CGELBank annotation, not LLM annotation.

human_adjudication.protocol: Two annotators independently annotated the 50-sentence EWT sample, received validator output after the initial pass, revised, and then jointly adjudicated disagreements.
human_adjudication.notes: The paper reports a five-sentence pilot before the 50-sentence study and cooperative adjudication using a text editor with file-comparison mode.

instruments.version: Reynolds and Schneider annotator pair in Reynolds, Arora, and Schneider 2023

settings.annotation_manual: CGELBank documentation, approximately 75 pages in the 2023 report
settings.annotation_tooling: Active DOP plus CGELBank .cgel workflow as reported

instruments.notes: The publication date is used as the validation date for this literature-summary record.

instruments.version: CGELBank validator as reported in Reynolds, Arora, and Schneider 2023

settings.checks: misspelled labels, phrases with no Head, unusual category positions, unnecessary nesting, modifier branching, gap coindexation, coordination structure

instruments.notes: The exact validator version is not restated in this registry record; see the CGELBank repository and reported IAA output.

metrics.name: unlabelled-constituent-f1
metrics.interpretation: Microaveraged F1 for tree structure alone between the two annotators after validation, from Table 4.

metrics.name: flex-labelled-f1
metrics.interpretation: Main tree-edit-distance flex metric between the two annotators after validation, from Table 4.

metrics.name: strict-labelled-f1
metrics.interpretation: Strict labelled F1 between the two annotators after validation, from Table 4.

metrics.name: gap-f1
metrics.interpretation: Gap F1 between the two annotators after validation, from Table 4.

metrics.name: full-tree-exact-match
metrics.interpretation: Percentage of trees identical between the two annotators after validation, from Table 4.

metrics.name: validator-effect-annotator-agreement
metrics.interpretation: The paper reports improved flex agreement between annotators after validator feedback.

metrics.name: validator-effect-a1-to-adjudicated
metrics.interpretation: The paper reports improved agreement between annotator 1 and the adjudicated trees after validator feedback.

metrics.name: validator-effect-a2-to-adjudicated
metrics.interpretation: The paper reports improved agreement between annotator 2 and the adjudicated trees after validator feedback.

disagreement_analysis.description: Among aligned nodes in the flex comparison, function disagreements were more numerous than category disagreements.
disagreement_analysis.action: Keep category/function separation explicit and track function disagreements as their own error class.

disagreement_analysis.description: The published cost breakdown reports category disagreements among substitution costs.
disagreement_analysis.action: Report category disagreements separately from function disagreements in future validation records.

disagreement_analysis.description: The paper reports lower gap F1 and identifies a shared-object coordination case as a major source of disagreement.
disagreement_analysis.action: Treat gapping and displacement as a named failure zone rather than assuming core-category agreement transfers automatically.

disagreement_analysis.description: The paper identifies structured names and measurements as qualitative sources of uncertainty and disagreement.
disagreement_analysis.action: Version construction-specific policies for names, measurements, currency, age, and temperature expressions.

disagreement_analysis.description: Insertion/deletion costs and validator warnings reflect attachment differences and missing or extra projection layers.
disagreement_analysis.action: Keep projection-spine rules and validator output attached to any downstream conformance check.

calibration_notes: The flex metric is the main reported metric because it gives partial credit for category, function, lexical, and gap-antecedent components.
Full-tree exact match is much stricter than the flex metric and should not be used alone as the reliability summary.
Validator output improved agreement but was not treated as automatic adjudication.

downstream_checks.name: trained-human CGELBank annotation consistency
downstream_checks.result: The 2023 paper reports flex F1 of 93.9 between annotators after validation on 50 EWT sentences.

downstream_checks.name: LLM or parser instrument validation
downstream_checks.result: No LLM or parser validation is claimed in this registry record.

downstream_checks.name: treebank/parser conformance check
downstream_checks.result: A future record should rerun validator and scorer outputs against a pinned CGELBank version.

scope_update.summary: The validation supports a reviewed core package for trained-human CGELBank annotation. It does not expand the package to full CGELBank, automatic conversion, LLM annotation, or parser performance.

artifacts.description: CGELBank public repository cited in the documentation and 2023 paper.
IAA scorer output URL reported in Reynolds, Arora, and Schneider 2023.

artifacts.path_or_uri: Reynolds, Arora, and Schneider 2023, Proceedings of LAW-XVII, pp. 220-234
artifacts.description: Source for annotation process, validation, IAA metrics, and qualitative disagreement discussion.

artifacts.path_or_uri: CGELBank documentation PDF in local CGELbank project folder
artifacts.description: Source for category/function/projection-spine policies summarized in this package.

conclusion.validity_scope: Reviewed literature-summary validation for trained-human CGELBank core category/function/projection annotation on English treebank sentences.
limitations: The registry record summarizes published results rather than rerunning the scorer.
The package covers the core representation only, not every CGELBank construction policy.
No LLM, API-model, or local-model instrument has been validated.
Gap analysis, structured names, measurements, and attachment/projection-layer decisions remain named failure zones.
